title: Automation matrix — 8/14 fulfillment tasks fully automatable
html:
A late-night strategic worksheet inside the Rankett admin context, classifying every fulfillment task by automation potential. Three buckets: Fully automated (cron, zero human) — AI Visibility Audit, Crawlability Check (foundational scorer), Schema Generation (template from AI Profile), llms.txt generation (template from AI Profile), CF Crawl Site Intelligence (one API call), Query Tracking (cron), Blog Refresh (cron, low-risk targeted edits), Internal Linking (algorithmic). Semi-auto needing review — AEO Blog Posts (generate + queue as drafts), Semantic Refresh (auto-refresh + flag), Snippet Engineering (identify targets + suggest fixes). Truly manual — GBP Optimization (Google login, no API), Featured.com Citations (account access required), YouTube Channel Setup (one-time, requires Google account). The crystallized insight at the bottom: "This means at 100 clients, ~80% of fulfillment could be a background job that runs itself. The admin dashboard is the control plane for an autonomous fulfillment engine."

TL;DR

  • 8 of 14 Rankett fulfillment tasks classified as fully automatable via cron — the autonomous fulfillment engine thesis crystallizes here.
  • 3 tasks are semi-auto (Claude generates, human reviews); 3 are truly manual (require Google logins / human pitching).
  • At 100 clients, ~80% of fulfillment runs without anyone touching it — the admin dashboard becomes the control plane, not the workshop.
  • Pattern names: "AI Profile" as the canonical client-data store from which llms.txt and schema markup are templated.

rankett · autonomous fulfillment engine · ai visibility engine · ghost white label · llm txt · schema markup for ai · no runway discipline
